title: Initialize `vietmap_flutter_gl` with API key (env var `VIETMAP_API_KEY`)
description:
Initialize flutter_map with a tile source using the API key injected via dart-define at build time.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. A MapScreen widget exists at lib/features/map/map_screen.dart that renders a flutter_map widget
2. The map is centered on Ho Chi Minh City (lat: 10.7769, lng: 106.7009) with zoom 13
3. The tile URL template uses Env.vietmapApiKey for the API key parameter; falls back to OpenStreetMap tiles when the key is empty (test mode)
4. MapScreen is registered on route '/map' in the GoRouter (route '/' stays as HomePage placeholder)
5. Widget test at test/features/map/map_screen_test.dart: MapScreen renders without crashing in test mode (empty API key -> OSM fallback)
6. No new dart-define keys are needed — VIETMAP_API_KEY already declared in Env

title: Bootstrap Flutter scaffolding (monorepo, DI, router, env)
description:
Bootstrap the Flutter monorepo scaffolding required before any CAPP-2/CAPP-4/CAPP-5 feature stories can be implemented. Foundation-only — no feature screens.

Spec: /Users/trungnguyenhoang/IdeaProjects/se-sport-buddies/systems/sportbuddies/CAPP/flutter-mobile-app-technical-plan-phase-1.md (v3, May 2026)

## Scope

Bring the repo from "grava harness only" to "runnable Flutter Web shell that boots, initializes Firebase + Supabase, sets up DI + router, and shows a placeholder home screen". No auth UI, no map, no booking — those land in their own CAPP stories.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **Monorepo layout** matches §1.3 of tech plan:
  - `packages/spb_core/` — pure Dart package with `pubspec.yaml` (no Flutter, no Supabase deps), folder skeleton: `entities/`, `usecases/`, `repositories/`, `core/{result.dart, failures.dart, value_objects/}`
  - `apps/customer/` — Flutter app with `pubspec.yaml`, folder skeleton: `lib/core/{di,router,env,theme,l10n}/`, `lib/features/`, `lib/main.dart`
- [ ] **`spb_core` package** compiles standalone (`dart analyze` clean) and exports:
  - `Result<T>` sealed class (`result.dart` per §7.1)
  - `AppFailure` sealed base + initial subclasses: `AuthFailure`, `NetworkFailure`, `ServerFailure` (per §7.2 — others added by feature stories)
  - `LatLng` value object with HCMC default constant `LatLng(10.776, 106.701)` (per §4.3)
- [ ] **`apps/customer` dependencies** in pubspec: `flutter_bloc ^8`, `supabase_flutter ^2`, `go_router ^14`, `flutter_map ^7`, `get_it`, `injectable`, `shared_preferences`, `firebase_core`, `flutter_localizations`, `intl`, `geolocator`, `vietmap_flutter_plugin`. Dev: `bloc_test`, `mocktail`, `build_runner`, `injectable_generator`.
- [ ] **`Env` class** (`core/env/env.dart`) reads via `String.fromEnvironment` for: `SUPABASE_URL`, `SUPABASE_ANON_KEY`, `VIETMAP_API_KEY`. No fallback defaults for secrets.
- [ ] **`main.dart` init order** matches §9.2 exactly: `WidgetsFlutterBinding.ensureInitialized()` → `Firebase.initializeApp` → `Supabase.initialize` → `SharedPreferences.getInstance` → `configureDependencies(prefs)` → `runApp(CustomerApp())`.
- [ ] **DI module** (`core/di/injection.dart`) using `get_it` + `injectable`:
  - `SupabaseClient` registered as Singleton
  - `SharedPreferences` registered as Singleton
  - `GoRouter` registered as Singleton
  - `build_runner` codegen runs clean (`dart run build_runner build --delete-conflicting-outputs`)

comments.message: Implementation complete. Commit: a48376a. Ready for code review.

[HIGH] review a48376a — 1 finding(s)

- test/core/di/injection_test.dart:18 — configureDependencies() is never called in any test. The test named 'SharedPreferences is registered when passed to configureDependencies' bypasses the function under test and registers the singleton manually. If configureDependencies had a bug (e.g., sl.init() omitted, wrong type registered), all 3 tests would still pass. This gives a false sense of coverage. Fix: add a test that calls configureDependencies(prefs) directly using a mock or by resetting and re-initialising GetIt, accepting that SupabaseClient/GoRouter accessors may throw so they can be caught — at minimum verify that SharedPreferences is resolvable after the call completes.

[MEDIUM] review a48376a — 2 finding(s)

- lib/core/di/injection_module.dart:22 — GoRouter(routes: const []) stub creates a live singleton with no routes. Any code that injects GoRouter before task 6 lands will receive a router that silently accepts navigation calls and goes nowhere, rather than throwing a clear error. Consider using an @Named annotation or an empty-route guard that asserts in debug mode, so the stub failure is loud rather than silent. The TODO comment is present and sufficient for now, but a debug-mode assertion would reduce surprise.

- lib/core/di/injection.config.dart — generated file committed to version control without a notice in analysis_options.yaml or .gitignore explaining it is intentionally tracked. Developers editing injection_module.dart without re-running build_runner will silently get a stale config. Add a comment at the top of injection.dart or a CONTRIBUTING note clarifying that build_runner must be re-run after any module change.

[LOW] review a48376a — 2 finding(s)

- lib/core/di/injection.dart:26 — sl.init() return value is discarded. The generated init() returns GetIt (not a Future), so no await is needed and discarding it is safe. However adding a comment like '// init() returns GetIt (not a Future) — no await needed' removes any ambiguity for future readers who might wonder if the missing await is a bug.

- test/core/di/injection_test.dart:25 — comment reads 'skip the full init and just verify the manual registration path that fires before sl.init()' but no rationale explains why the full init is skipped (i.e., Supabase.instance not available in unit test context). Add a brief explanation so future maintainers understand this is an intentional constraint, not an oversight.

[REVIEW] a48376a — 7 files, critical=0 high=1 medium=2 low=2

Summary: Adds the DI bootstrap layer (get_it + injectable) with injection.dart, injection_module.dart, the generated injection.config.dart, and 3 unit tests. The structure correctly satisfies all acceptance criteria — singletons for SupabaseClient, SharedPreferences, and GoRouter are registered, the §6.2 rule is documented, and build_runner output is committed. The main gap is that the tests never invoke configureDependencies() itself, so the primary function under test has zero direct test coverage.

Verdict: CHANGES_REQUESTED

[LOW] review 5cb6969 — 1 finding(s)

- test/core/di/injection_test.dart:32 — catch (_) swallows all exceptions, not just StateError. If configureDependencies throws for an unexpected reason (e.g., a bug introduced later in the registration sequence before sl.init()), the test would still pass. Consider narrowing to 'catch (e) { if (e is! StateError) rethrow; }' to keep the test honest against unexpected failures. Low severity because the documented constraint (Supabase unavailable in tests) is the only realistic exception source in this file today.

[REVIEW] 5cb6969 — 1 files, critical=0 high=0 medium=0 low=1

Summary: Fix round 1 adds a direct call to configureDependencies(prefs) in a new test, catching the expected Supabase StateError and asserting SharedPreferences is registered. This fully resolves the prior HIGH finding (no direct coverage of configureDependencies). The remaining MEDIUM/LOW findings from round 1 (GoRouter stub silence, generated file stale-config risk, await comment) are unchanged in the original files and do not block merge.

Verdict: APPROVED

[LOW] review a961fed — 2 finding(s)

- firebase.json:2 — `hosting` is declared as an object with `"target": "customer"`. Valid for single-site, but the conventional multi-site shape (which `.firebaserc` targets imply) is `"hosting": [{ "target": "customer", ... }]` (array). Current form deploys fine with `firebase deploy --only hosting:customer` (matches CLAUDE.md). If a second hosting site is ever added, this will need to be migrated to the array form. Suggested fix: switch to array form now to future-proof, or leave a TODO comment outside the JSON (e.g., in deploy docs).
- apps/customer/lib/firebase_options.dart:73-77 — placeholders set `authDomain` as a non-null `TODO-...` string, while the real flutterfire-generated file leaves optional fields null for platforms that don't need them. Harmless (file is regenerated wholesale by `flutterfire configure`), but downstream code reading `options.authDomain` against `!= null` will see a truthy placeholder, not absence. Suggested fix (optional): leave optional placeholders as `null` so absence-semantics match the real generated file.

[REVIEW] a961fed — 4 files, critical=0 high=0 medium=0 low=2
